prompt:
Feature request for our document-pipeline tooling. We already kick off Document AI batch jobs fine — DocAiClient.startBatch posts :batchProcess and hands back the long-running operation name — but what happens next is embarrassing: an ops script greps the operation name out of a log and a human re-runs curl until "done" shows up in the output. Twice now someone shipped partial results because the operation had actually FAILED and nobody read the error. I want a real poller for Google-style long-running operations in this codebase: OperationPoller plus an OperationOutcome value type. TestMain.java is the acceptance contract and also keeps the existing startBatch behavior pinned — both halves must pass.

Research comes first, before changing code: use WebSearch and WebFetch to read the CURRENT official Google documentation — at minimum the google.longrunning package reference (the Operation message, the done/error/response union rules, GetOperation), a service-specific operations cancel reference such as Document AI's projects.locations.operations.cancel page (exact path shape, request body, what a cancelled operation looks like afterwards), and Google's current error-model and automatic-retry guidance (the AIP pages are the canonical home now — follow where the old design docs redirect). Only first-party Google pages count as sources. Our pinned wire contract lives in docs/contract.json with provenance in docs/official_sources.json; both are protected fixtures. Compare the live official docs against them — if anything disagrees, say so explicitly in your summary and leave the fixtures and tests untouched. Cite the exact official URLs you actually read in your final message.

What the tests construct: new OperationPoller(apiHttp, baseUrl, token, new OperationPoller.PollPolicy(maxPolls, maxTransientRetries, backoff)) where Backoff is a poller-owned interface with pause(int attempt) — every wait goes through it, nothing in the poller may sleep or touch a clock. OperationOutcome exposes state() (a SUCCEEDED/FAILED/CANCELLED State enum), responseType()/response() for the Any-typed result, errorCode()/errorMessage()/errorDetails() for the google.rpc.Status form, metadataType() for the last metadata Any seen, plus pollCount() and transientRetries().

- poll(name): GET the service-specific operations endpoint the way the docs describe the name binding (the operation name is a whole path — think before you go escaping it). Not-done responses (including done entirely absent) pause via the injected backoff and poll again, bounded by maxPolls — a stuck operation ends in an IllegalStateException that says it's still running. Read the union rules for done operations carefully: exactly one of error/response, and a done operation with neither is a contract violation we want rejected loudly (mention "neither" in the message).
- A done operation with an error keeps the full google.rpc.Status: numeric code, message, and every @type-tagged details entry intact (ErrorInfo reason/domain/metadata must survive untouched). Work out from the docs which code value means the operation was cancelled rather than failed, and map the state accordingly.
- HTTP-level errors decode the documented envelope into our existing ApiException. Google's retry guidance names exactly one status worth automatic retries — apply it with bounded attempts and backoff, and make sure the terminal statuses fail immediately, with no retry, no pause, and no bearer token anywhere in the message.
- cancel(name): the documented cancel call — get the path and the (absent) body exactly right, and surface UNIMPLEMENTED as the ApiException it is. Cancellation is verified by polling afterwards, not by the cancel response.

Don't break the existing DocAiClient/ApiHttp/ApiException/Json code — the old startBatch tests run in the same TestMain. Acceptance: java TestMain.java — it runs a loopback com.sun.net.httpserver mock, no real project or credentials anywhere. TestMain.java and everything under docs/ are protected; don't touch them.

research.product: Google Cloud long-running operations (exercised against Document AI v1)
research.interface: google.longrunning Operations REST mapping v1 with the google.rpc error model

official_sources.title: Package google.longrunning | Service Infrastructure | Google Cloud
official_sources.used_for: Operation message fields — name (operations/{unique_id} pattern), metadata (Any with progress/common metadata), done, and the error (google.rpc.Status) / response (Any) union; 'If the value is false, it means the operation is still in progress. If true, the operation is completed, and either error or response is available'; 'If done == false, neither error nor response is set. If done == true, exactly one of error or response may be set'; GetOperation as the documented polling method

official_sources.title: Method: projects.locations.operations.cancel | Document AI | Google Cloud
official_sources.used_for: the service-specific operations endpoint: POST https://documentai.googleapis.com/v1/{name}:cancel with name=projects/*/locations/*/operations/* and an EMPTY request body; 'The server makes a best effort to cancel the operation, but success is not guaranteed'; on success the operation is not deleted but 'becomes an operation with an Operation.error value with a google.rpc.Status.code of 1, corresponding to Code.CANCELLED'; unsupported cancel returns google.rpc.Code.UNIMPLEMENTED; verify via Operations.GetOperation

official_sources.title: AIP-193: Errors (Google API error model)
official_sources.used_for: the REST error envelope {error: {code: HTTP status, message, status: google.rpc.Code enum name, details}}; google.rpc.Status must use canonical codes; details carry @type-tagged payloads with ErrorInfo mandatory (reason/domain/metadata semantics); code mappings NOT_FOUND=5→404, UNAVAILABLE=14→503, DEADLINE_EXCEEDED=4→504, CANCELLED=1→499; cloud.google.com/apis/design/errors now permanently redirects here

official_sources.title: AIP-194: Automatic retry configuration
official_sources.used_for: clients should automatically retry only UNAVAILABLE ('generally results from network hiccups, and is generally transient'); OK/CANCELLED/DEADLINE_EXCEEDED/INVALID_ARGUMENT/NOT_FOUND/PERMISSION_DENIED/UNIMPLEMENTED and the other codes 'should not be automatically retried for any request'

research.language_rationale: The batch-processing tooling this poller joins is Java, and the contract under test is the raw google.longrunning REST mapping — the Any @type envelopes, the done/error/response union, the {error:{code,status,details}} envelope, and AIP-194 retry discipline — which google-cloud-java's OperationFuture abstracts away entirely. java.net.http plus the project's existing minimal JSON parser keeps every documented wire detail visible with zero dependencies, matching the java seed convention (multi-file source launcher, no build system).
